questions.question: Namaste in Sanskrit means:
questions.explanation: Namaste (नमस्ते): respectful greeting, literally "I bow to you"

questions.question: Kannada script belongs to which family?
questions.explanation: Kannada uses its own script derived from ancient Brahmi

questions.question: How do you say "thank you" in Hindi?
questions.explanation: Both Dhanyavaad (formal Sanskrit-derived) and Shukriya (Urdu-influenced) mean thank you

questions.question: ¿Cómo estás? in Spanish means:
questions.explanation: ¿Cómo estás? = How are you? (informal)

questions.question: Sanskrit is written in which script?
questions.explanation: Sanskrit uses Devanagari script (देवनागरी), also used for Hindi

questions.question: Kannada word for "water":
questions.explanation: Neer (ನೀರು) is water in Kannada

questions.question: Hindi word for "book":
questions.explanation: Both Pustak (Sanskrit-origin) and Kitaab (Persian-origin) mean book in Hindi

questions.question: "Buenos días" means:
questions.explanation: Buenos días = Good morning/day (literally "good days")

questions.question: Sanskrit "Shanti" (शान्ति) means:
questions.explanation: Shanti = peace, tranquility. Often chanted 3 times for complete peace.

questions.question: How many noun genders in Spanish?
questions.explanation: Spanish nouns are masculine (el) or feminine (la)

questions.question: Kannada: Dhanyavāda (ಧನ್ಯವಾದ) means:
questions.explanation: Dhanyavāda = thank you in Kannada

questions.question: Hindi "Mera naam __ hai" means:
questions.explanation: Mera naam [X] hai = My name is [X]

questions.question: "Por favor" in Spanish means:
questions.explanation: Por favor = please (literally "by favor")

questions.question: Sanskrit for "knowledge":
questions.explanation: Both Vidya and Jnana mean knowledge, Jnana often implies deeper wisdom

questions.question: Kannada is primarily spoken in which state?
questions.explanation: Kannada is official language of Karnataka state

questions.question: Hindi: Kaise ho? (कैसे हो?) means:
questions.explanation: Kaise ho? = How are you? (informal)

questions.question: "De nada" in Spanish means:
questions.explanation: De nada = You are welcome (literally "of nothing")

questions.question: Sanskrit: Guru (गुरु) literally means:
questions.explanation: Guru: "heavy, weighty" - one who dispels ignorance (darkness)

questions.question: Kannada: Namaskaara (ನಮಸ್ಕಾರ) is:
questions.explanation: Namaskaara = respectful greeting in Kannada

questions.question: "Adiós" means:
questions.explanation: Adiós = goodbye (from "a Dios" - to God)

questions.question: Sanskrit sandhi rule: "a + i" becomes:
questions.explanation: Sandhi: a + i → e (vowel combination rule)

questions.question: Kannada verb "maaDu" (ಮಾಡು) means:
questions.explanation: maaDu = to do, to make (common verb)

questions.question: Hindi past tense marker for masculine:
questions.explanation: Masculine singular past: verb stem + -aa (करना → किया)

questions.question: Spanish preterite "hablé" means:
questions.explanation: Preterite: completed past action. Hablar → hablé (I spoke)

questions.question: Sanskrit: Bhagavad Gita (भगवद्गीता) means:
questions.explanation: Bhagavad (divine/blessed) + Gita (song)

questions.question: Kannada word order is typically:
questions.explanation: Kannada is SOV: "I apple eat" not "I eat apple"

questions.question: Hindi: Postpositions vs Prepositions:
questions.explanation: Hindi: "ghar mein" (house in) not "in house" - postposition

questions.question: Spanish subjunctive expresses:
questions.explanation: Subjunctive: "Espero que vengas" (I hope you come) - uncertainty/desire

questions.question: Sanskrit vibhakti (case system) has how many cases?
questions.explanation: 8 cases: Nominative, Accusative, Instrumental, Dative, Ablative, Genitive, Locative, Vocative

questions.question: Kannada has __vowels:
questions.explanation: Kannada: a, ā, i, ī, u, ū, ṛ, ṝ, ḷ, ḹ, e, ē, ai, o, ō, au

questions.question: Hindi ergative construction in:
questions.explanation: Ergative: "Maine khana khaya" (I-by food eat-past) - subject takes "ne"

questions.question: Spanish "ser" vs "estar":
questions.explanation: Soy doctor (permanent), Estoy cansado (temporary state)

questions.question: Sanskrit meter: Anushtubh shloka has:
questions.explanation: Anushtubh: most common meter, 4 padas of 8 syllables = 32 total

questions.question: Kannada: Gender distinction in:
questions.explanation: Kannada: limited gender, mainly in third-person pronouns (avanu/avaLu)

questions.question: Hindi: Tum vs Aap:
questions.explanation: Three levels: tuu (very informal), tum (casual), aap (respectful)

questions.question: Spanish "gustar" means literally:
questions.explanation: "Me gusta" = "It is pleasing to me" (indirect object construction)

questions.question: Sanskrit: Samasa (compound) types:
questions.explanation: 6 types: Avyayibhava, Tatpurusha, Karmadharaya, Dvigu, Dvandva, Bahuvrihi

questions.question: Kannada: "alli" (ಅಲ್ಲಿ) means:
questions.explanation: alli = there, illi = here, elli = where

questions.question: Hindi: Oblique case is used:
questions.explanation: Oblique: "laRka" (nom) → "laRke ne" (oblique + postposition)

questions.question: Spanish: "haber" as auxiliary:
questions.explanation: Haber: perfect tenses. "He comido" = I have eaten

questions.question: Bhagavad Gita 2.47: "Karmanye vadhikaraste..."
options: You have right to action, but not to fruits of action
questions.explanation: Famous verse on selfless action (Karma Yoga)

questions.question: Sanskrit Panini grammar: Ashtadhyayi has how many sutras?
questions.explanation: ~3959-4000 sutras: most comprehensive ancient grammar

questions.question: Kannada classical literature: Pampa wrote:
questions.explanation: Pampa (10th century): Father of Kannada literature, wrote Adipurana

questions.question: Hindi literary movement: Chhayavaad (छायावाद) was:
questions.explanation: Chhayavaad: romantic poetry by Prasad, Pant, Nirala, Mahadevi Varma

questions.question: Spanish Golden Age writer: Cervantes wrote:
questions.explanation: Don Quixote: foundational work of Western literature

questions.question: Sanskrit Vedas: oldest Veda is:
questions.explanation: Rigveda: oldest religious text, collection of 1028 hymns

questions.question: Kannada Vachana sahitya composed by:
questions.explanation: Vachanas: devotional poetry in simple Kannada by Basavanna, Akka Mahadevi

questions.question: Hindi poet Kabir wrote in:
questions.explanation: Kabir: 15th century mystic poet, used common language (Bhasha)

questions.question: Spanish: Voseo (used in Argentina):
options: Informal you: vos instead of tú
questions.explanation: Voseo: vos hablás (instead of tú hablas) in Rioplatense Spanish

questions.question: Sanskrit: Upanishads focus on:
questions.explanation: Upanishads: Vedanta philosophy, "tat tvam asi" (thou art that)

questions.question: Kannada: Rashtrakuta inscription language:
questions.explanation: Kavirajamarga (850 CE): oldest extant Kannada literary work

questions.question: Hindi Urdu controversy centered on:
questions.explanation: Hindi-Urdu: same grammar, different scripts and formal registers

questions.question: Spanish: Subjunctive in "si" (if) clauses:
questions.explanation: "Si tuviera dinero..." (If I had money...) - imperfect subjunctive

questions.question: Sanskrit sandhi: Visarga + voiced → ?
questions.explanation: Visarga sandhi: complex rules, often becomes "r" before voiced

questions.question: Kannada: Halegannada refers to:
questions.explanation: Halegannada: inscriptions and early literature before medieval period

questions.question: Hindi: Tulsidas Ramcharitmanas language:
questions.explanation: Ramcharitmanas: Awadhi retelling of Ramayana (16th century)

questions.question: Spanish: Catalán vs Castellano:
questions.explanation: Catalán: distinct Romance language (not dialect) in Catalonia

questions.question: Sanskrit: Yoga Sutras of Patanjali has:
questions.explanation: Yoga Sutras: ~196 aphorisms, foundational yogic philosophy

questions.question: Kannada: Jnanpith Award winners include:
options: Eight winners (Kuvempu, Bendre, U.R. Ananthamurthy, etc.)
questions.explanation: Kannada has won Jnanpith (Indias highest literary honor) 8 times

questions.question: Hindi: Premchand considered father of:
questions.explanation: Munshi Premchand: pioneering realist fiction (Godaan, Kafan)
